---
format: 
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    theme: [default, custom_theme.scss]
    preview-links: auto
    output-file: "presentation_01"
    output-ext: "html"
    logo: "../../../../Documentation/Materials/Logos/BIODYNAMICS.png"
    code-line-numbers: false
    code-overflow: scroll
    code-link: false
    embed-resources: true
    center-title-slide: false
    include-in-header: "fonts-include.html"
    pdf-separate-fragments: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
#| eval: true
library(here)

here::i_am("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/_presentation_01.qmd")

source(
  here::here("R/___setup_project___.R")
)

library(jsonlite)
library(qrcode)
library(Hmsc)
library(MCMCvis)
library(tinytable)
library(waffle)
library(bipartite)

path_to_materials <-
  here::here("Documentation/Materials")

include_local_figure <- function(data_source) {
  knitr::include_graphics(
    path = here::here(
      path_to_materials,
      data_source
    ),
    error = TRUE
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: dynamic-theme
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| message: false

# Colors
colors <-
  jsonlite::fromJSON(
    here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/colors.json")
  )

colors_definition <-
  purrr::imap(
    .x = colors,
    .f = ~ {
      paste0(
        "$", .y, ": ", unname(.x), ";", "\n",
        ".reveal .bg-", .y, " { background-color: ", unname(.x), "; }\n",
        ".text-color-", .y, " { color: ", unname(.x), " !important ; }\n",
        ".text-background-", .y, " {\n",
        "background-color: ", unname(.x), ";\n",
        "padding: $smallMargin;\n",
        "border-radius: 5px;\n",
        " }", "\n"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  paste(collapse = "\n")


writeLines(
  text = c(
    "// This file is auto-generated from colors.json. Do not edit directly.",
    colors_definition
  ),
  con = here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/_colors.scss")
)

# Fonts

fonts <-
  jsonlite::fromJSON(
    here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/fonts.json")
  )

fonts_definition <-
  c(
    paste0('$mainFont: "', fonts$body, '", "Arial", sans-serif !default;\n'),
    paste0('$headingFont: "', fonts$heading, '", "Courier New", monospace !default;\n'),
    paste0(
      ".text-font-body { font-family: $mainFont; }\n",
      ".text-font-heading { font-family: $headingFont; }\n"
    ),
    paste0(
      "/* Debug font loading - this will help us see if the font is loaded */\n",
      "@supports (font-family: \"", fonts$heading, "\") {\n",
      "  .debug-font-loaded::before {\n",
      "    content: \"", fonts$heading, " font is supported\";\n",
      "    display: block;\n",
      "    font-size: 12px;\n",
      "    color: green;\n",
      "  }\n",
      "}\n\n",
      "/* Force font loading for debugging */\n",
      ".force-press-start {\n",
      "  font-family: \"", fonts$heading, "\", monospace !important;\n",
      "  font-display: swap;\n",
      "}\n"
    )
  )

writeLines(
  text = c(
    "// This file is auto-generated from fonts.json. Do not edit directly.",
    fonts_definition
  ),
  con = here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/_fonts.scss")
)
```

# {.bg-green .text-color-white}

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="37%"}
:::

::: {.column width="25%"}

```{r}
#| label: biodynamics-logo-0
include_local_figure("Logos/BIODYNAMICS.png")
```

:::

::: {.column width="37%"}
:::

::::

<br>

:::: {.r-hstack}
::: {data-id="box1" style="background: `r colors$white`; width: 120px; height: 15px; margin: 0px;"}
:::

::: {data-id="box2" style="background: `r colors$pink`; width: 550px; height: 15px; margin: 0px;"}
:::

::: {data-id="box3" style="background: `r colors$black`; width: 120px; height: 15px; margin: 0px;"}
:::

::: {data-id="box3" style="background: `r colors$blue`; width: 120px; height: 15px; margin: 0px;"}
:::
::::

::: {.text-color-white .text-font-heading .text-size-heading3}
[BIODYNAMICS]{.text-bold .text-color-black}: Spatio-temporal dynamics of biotic interactions
:::

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}

:::


::: {.column width="60%" .text-right .text-color-black .text-size-body}
[👤](https://ondrejmottl.github.io/)[Ondřej Mottl]{.text-color-white}

[20.11.2025]{.text-color-black}

:::

::::

## BIODYNAMICS

- 3 years project (2023-2025) \[extended to 2026 due to parental leave\]
- Funded by Czech Science Foundation (GAČR)

### Objectives

::: {.incremental}

1. [Assemble an **interdisciplinary database** that integrates present and past vegetation records with their functional traits and abiotic environments since the Last Glacial Maximum]{.text-color-blue} (✅)
2. Identify how [**biotic interactions**]{.text-background-pink .text-bold} scale up to form and **maintain biodiversity patterns** at various [spatial/temporal/taxonomic]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} scales 
3. [Improve the capacity to **predict global biodiversity patterns** by including scale-dependent information about biotic interactions]{.text-color-blue} (maybe❔)

:::

# VegVault

:::::: {.columns}

::::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| label: VegVault logo
include_local_figure("Logos/VegVault_logo.png")
```

:::::

::::: {.column width="50%" .incremental}

* A global SQLite database linking [interdisciplinary]{.text-background-green .text-bold} **plot-based** vegetation data! 
* Links to functional **traits**, **climate** variables, and soil properties 
* Built for exploring **biodiversity** dynamics across [time & space]{.text-background-blue .text-bold}
* Open Source & [Reproducible]{.text-background-brown .text-bold}
* 🔗Learn more: [bit.ly/VegVault](https://bit.ly/VegVault)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

```{r}
#| label: vegvault_qr_code
qr_vegvault <-
  qrcode::qr_code(
    "https://bit.ly/VegVault",
    ecl = "H"
  )

qrcode::generate_svg(
  qrcode = qr_vegvault,
  file = here::here(
    path_to_materials,
    "QR/qr_vegvault.svg"),
  foreground = colors$black,
  background = colors$beigeLight
)

include_local_figure("QR/qr_vegvault.svg")
```

:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
:::

::::

:::::

::::::


## VegVault - Overview

~110 GB | 32 tables & 87 variables | 480,000+ Datasets | 13M+ Samples  | 110,000+ Taxa | 11M+ Trait values | 8 Abiotic variables

:::: {.columns .fragment}

#### Data sources 

::: {.column width="40%" .incremental}

- 🌿 [**Paleoecological records**]{.text-background-brown .text-bold} ([Neotoma]{.text-italic}, [FOSSILPOL]{.text-italic})
- 🌱 [**Contemporary vegetation**]{.text-background-green .text-bold} ([sPlotOpen]{.text-italic}, [BIEN]{.text-italic})
- 🌾 **Functional traits** ([BIEN]{.text-italic}, [TRY]{.text-italic})
- 🌍 **Climate & soil data** ([CHELSA]{.text-italic}, [WoSIS]{.text-italic})  
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}

```{r}
#| label: Scheme
include_local_figure("VegVault/assembly_viz_04.png")
```
:::

::::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

## Key Innovations

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.text-size-heading3 .text-bold .fragment fragment-index=1}
1. **Vegetation** data
:::

::: {.incremental fragment-index=3}

- Integration of paleo and contemporary data
- [Plot-based]{.text-background-green .text-bold} vegetation records 
   + not just species occurrences
   + [true negatives!]{.text-background-pink .text-bold}

:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%" .fragment fragment-index=2}

| Sample ID | Taxa A | Taxa B |
|---|--------|--------|
| 1 | 🔬🟡 | 🔬🟡🟡🟡 |
| 2 | 🟩🌱🌱🌱 | |
| 3 | 🟩🌱🌱 | 🟩🌱🌱 |

:::

::::

<!-- Slide 2 -->

## Key Innovations

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

::: {.text-size-heading3 .text-bold}
1. **Abiotic** context
:::

::: {.incremental fragment-index=1}

- [Sample-specific]{.text-background-green .text-bold} abiotic data
   + mapped to spatio-temporal grids
   + paleo and contemporary 
- Selected variables for [vegetation studies]{.text-background-brown .text-bold}
   + e.g. temperature, precipitation, soil type, etc. 

:::

:::

::: {.column width="40%" .fragment fragment-index=2}

```{r}
#| label: link of abiotic data spatial example
include_local_figure("VegVault/plot_abiotic_example_spatial.png")
```
:::

::::

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="20%"}
:::


::: {.column width="50%" .fragment fragment-index=2}
```{r}
#| label: link of abiotic data temporal example
include_local_figure("VegVault/plot_abiotic_example_temporal.png")
```

:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
:::

::::

::: {.footer}
Figures from [VegVault presentation](https://ondrejmottl.github.io/VegVault-Presentation-20250802/)
:::

<!-- Slide 3 -->

## Key Innovations

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.text-size-heading3 .text-bold .fragment fragment-index=1}
3. **Standardisation**
:::

::: {.incremental fragment-index=2}

- [Trait domains]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} 
    + pooling of traits across sources
    + Following [Diaz et al. (2016)]{.text-italic .text-background-green .text-color-white} 

:::

::: {.incremental fragment-index=4}

- [Taxonomic harmonisation]{.text-background-pink .text-bold} via GBIF backbone and `{taxospace}`📦  
   + unified taxonomy across dataset vegetation, fossil pollen and traits

:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%" .fragment fragment-index=3}
Trait source |Trait name| Trait Domain|
|---|---|---|
| BIEN | [leaf nitrogen content per leaf dry mass]{.text-italic} | [Leaf nitrogen content per unit mass]{.text-italic .text-underline} |
| TRY | [Leaf nitrogen (N) content per leaf dry mass]{.text-italic} | [Leaf nitrogen content per unit mass]{.text-italic .text-underline} |

:::

::::

# Biotic interactions

<br>

::: {.incremental}

 - [Non-random cooccurrences]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} after accounting for environmental filtering 
- Can be positive (facilitation) or negative (competition)
- Are [scale dependent]{.text-background-green .text-bold} (?)

:::

## Scales (3 axes)

<br>

::: {.incremental}

- **Spatial**
  - “_Biotic interactions from individual sites (local) to whole continents (global)_”
- **Temporal**
  - “_currently observed changes in biodiversity began millennia ago_”
- **Taxonomic**
  - individual taxa
  - phylogenetically cofound groups
  - groups occupying different ecological niches (i.e. Functional Types)

:::

## Research questions

<br>

1. Is there a [**scale dependence**]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} in the  amount of unexplained variation (potentially due to biotic interactions) structuring vegetation?

::: {.incremental}

 - Expectation 1 - Strength of biotic interactions decrease with increasing [spatial scale]{.text-background-green .text-bold}
 - Expectation 2 - The importance of biotic interaction increase in the periods of large changes (climatic - LGM, anthropogenic - last centuries)
 - Expectation 3 - low taxonomic resolution show stronger biotic interactions at larger spatial scales (interaction between spatial and taxonomic scale)

:::

## Research questions

<br>

2. Can we improve the [**predictive capacity**]{.text-background-pink .text-bold} of biodiversity models by including biotic interactions and historical data?

::: {.incremental}

 - Expectation 4 - Including [historical data]{.text-background-brown .text-bold} will improve model predictive capacity, especially at large scales
 - Expectation 5 - Including biotic interactions will improve model predictive capacity, especially at local scales

:::

# Methodological framework

<br>

To address the objectives and research questions, I am developing a large code base with [modular]{.text-background-green .text-bold} and [reusable]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} **data-analysis pipelines** 

```{r}
#| label: sjSDM
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
include_local_figure("BIODYNAMICS/sjSDM.png")
```

The main methodology is [Joined Species Distribution Modelling]{.text-background-pink .text-bold} (JSDM), which allows to infer biotic interactions from species cooccurrence patterns after accounting for environmental filtering

::: {.footer}
[{sjSDM}](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/sjSDM/vignettes/sjSDM_Introduction.html)
:::

## Hierarchical Modelling of Species Communities (HMSC) 

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="25%" .fragment}

```{r}
#| label: hmsc-book
include_local_figure("Hmsc/9781108716789i.jpg")
```

:::

::: {.column width="75%" .fragment}

```{r}
#| label: hmsc-viz
include_local_figure("Hmsc/61506-ele_ms_figures-1_3_4.png.webp")
```

:::

::::


## Methodological framework

```{r}
#| label: extract-codecov
#| echo: false 
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

var_codecov <-
  jsonlite::fromJSON(
    here::here("Documentation/Functions_test_coverage/covr_report_summary.json")
  )  %>% 
  purrr::chuck("value")
```

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| label: r-logo
include_local_figure("Logos/R_logo.svg")
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%" .incremental}
- using [R]{.text-background-blue} for all data processing and analyses
- [functional programming]{.text-background-green .text-bold} principles for modularity and reusability
  - everything is a _function_
  - each function is [tested and documented]{.text-background-brown .text-bold} ([example](https://ondrejmottl.github.io/BIODYNAMICS-vegetation-cooccurrence/website/Documentation/Functions/add_age_to_samples.html))
  - current code coverage: `r var_codecov`%
:::

::::

# "Project" + Pipeline

<br>

::: {.incremental}

 - to test the individual research questions, I am developing multiple [data-analysis _pipelines_]{.text-background-blue .text-bold}
   - each pipeline is a sequence of data processing and analysis steps
   - each pipeline is tailored to a specific research question
- Data and configuration are separated into ["project"s]{.text-background-green .text-bold}
   - each project corresponds to a specific data subset and configuration settings
   - e.g. different regions, temporal resolution, taxonomic resolutions, etc.

:::

## Basic Pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{r}
#| label: setup-pipeline-basic
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
Sys.setenv(R_CONFIG_ACTIVE = "project_cz")

vec_pipelines <- "pipeline_basic"

set_store <-
  vec_pipelines %>%
  paste0(
    get_active_config("target_store"), "/", ., "/"
  ) %>%
  here::here()
```

## Basic Pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-0
flowchart LR
    Project-.->Pipeline
    subgraph Project
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-1
flowchart LR
    Config-.->Analyses
    subgraph Project
    Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
    Analyses
    end
```


## Basic Pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-2
flowchart LR
    Config-.->Data
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        Data-->Model
        Model-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline - Data

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-3
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Model
        Model-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline - Data

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-3
```

## Basic Pipeline - Data

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-4
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_community
        VegVault-->Data_abiotic
        Data_community-->Model
        Data_abiotic-->Model
        Model-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline - Data

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-5
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Data_interpolated
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_community
        VegVault-->Data_abiotic
        Data_community-->Data_interpolated
        Data_abiotic-->Data_interpolated
        Data_interpolated-->Model
        Model-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline - Data

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-6
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Classification
    Config-.->Data_interpolated
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_community
        VegVault-->Data_abiotic
        Data_community-->Classification
        Classification-->Data_harmonised
        Data_abiotic-->Data_interpolated
        Data_harmonised-->Data_interpolated
        Data_interpolated-->Model
        Model-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline - Model

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-6
```

## Basic Pipeline - Model

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-7
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Classification
    Config-.->Data_interpolated
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_community
        VegVault-->Data_abiotic
        Data_community-->Classification
        Classification-->Data_harmonised
        Data_abiotic-->Data_interpolated
        Data_harmonised-->Data_interpolated
        Data_interpolated-->Model
        Model-->Model_evaluation
        Model_evaluation-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## Basic Pipeline - Model

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-8
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Classification
    Config-.->Data_interpolated
    Config-.->Model
    Config-.->Species_associations
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_community
        VegVault-->Data_abiotic
        Data_community-->Classification
        Classification-->Data_harmonised
        Data_abiotic-->Data_interpolated
        Data_harmonised-->Data_interpolated
        Data_interpolated-->Model
        Model-->Model_null
        Model-->Model_full
        Model_null-->Model_evaluation
        Model_full-->Model_evaluation
        Model_evaluation-->Species_associations
        Species_associations-->Results
    end
```

## R implementation

<br>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%" .fragment}

```{r}
#| label: targets-logo
include_local_figure("Logos/targets_logo.svg")
```
:::

::: {.column width="70%" .incremental}
- The pipeline is implemented using the `{targets}` R package 📦
  - Each step is a function producing [_targets_]{.text-background-green .text-bold}
  - Each pipeline is constructed using [_pipe segments_]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} 
    - this allows for modular and reusable code (see later slides)

:::

::::

## Basic Pipeline - {targets}

```{r}
#| label: target-graph-basic
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| output: false
plot_target_graph_basic <-
  targets::tar_visnetwork(
    script = paste0(
      "R/02_Main_analyses", "/", vec_pipelines, ".R"
    ) %>%
      here::here(),
    store = set_store,
    targets_only = TRUE,
    outdated = FALSE,
    physics = TRUE,
    level_separation = 150
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: target-graph-basic-plot;
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| output: true
plot_target_graph_basic
```

## "Project" == Czech Republic

<br>

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
mod_hmsc_with_eval <-
  targets::tar_read(
    name = "mod_hmsc_to_use",
    store = set_store
  )

mod_hmsc <-
  mod_hmsc_with_eval %>%
  purrr::chuck("mod")

n_samples <- get_active_config(
  value = c("model_fitting", "samples")
)

n_validation_folds <- get_active_config(
  value = c("model_fitting", "cross_validation_folds")
)

n_cores <- get_active_config(
  value = c("model_fitting", "n_cores")
)

n_samples_total <- n_samples * n_cores


char_abiotic_vars <-
  paste(
    get_active_config(
      c("vegvault_data", "sel_abiotic_var_name")
    ),
    collapse = ", "
  )

char_taxonomic_resolution <-
  get_active_config(
    c("data_processing", "taxonomic_resolution")
  )
```

::: {.incremental}

- simple and small
- used only to test the pipeline
- Settings:
  - only [paleo data]{.text-background-brown .text-bold}
  - time extent: 0-5 ka BP
  - abiotic variables: `r char_abiotic_vars`
  - taxonomic resolution: `r char_taxonomic_resolution`
  - model: `r n_cores` chains, `r n_samples` samples per chain (total `r n_samples_total`), `r n_validation_folds` cross-validation folds

:::

# Model validation and exploration

<br>

- [convergence diagnostics]{.text-background-blue .text-bold}
- comparison to null model
- [variation partitioning]{.text-background-green .text-bold}
- effect sizes
- species associations

## Convergence diagnostics

<br>

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-Rhat
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true

mod_beta_summary <-
  Hmsc::convertToCodaObject(mod_hmsc) %>%
  purrr::chuck("Beta") %>%
  MCMCvis::MCMCsummary() %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

mod_beta_summary %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    ggplot2::aes(x = rhat)
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "R-hat values",
    y = "Count",
    title = "R-hat values for the beta parameters of the model"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 1.1, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggplot2::xlim(1, NA)
```

Terrible model - this is just a toy example

## Comparison to null model

<br>

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-compare
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
#| cache: true
mod_hmsc_fitted_combined <-
  targets::tar_read(
    name = "mod_hmsc_fitted_combined",
    store = set_store
  )

mod_null_waic <-
  mod_hmsc_fitted_combined %>%
  purrr::chuck("mod_hmsc_eval_..1_null", "mod") %>%
  Hmsc::computeWAIC()

mod_full_waic <-
  mod_hmsc_fitted_combined %>%
  purrr::chuck("mod_hmsc_eval_..._full", "mod") %>%
  Hmsc::computeWAIC()
```

- I am selecting model based on the [WAIC value]{.text-background-green .text-bold} 
  - there is option to use ThurR2 as well
  - This is done automatically to select NULL or FULL model.

<br>

::: {.incremental}
- NULL: `r round(mod_null_waic, 3)`
- FULL: `r round(mod_full_waic, 3)`
:::

::: {.fragment}
Let's pretend that the FULL model is the one we want to show here.
:::

## Variation partitioning

<br>

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-varpart-data
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

mod_hmsc_full_with_eval <-
  targets::tar_read(
    name = "mod_hmsc_eval_..._full",
    store = set_store
  )

mod_hmsc_full <-
  mod_hmsc_full_with_eval %>%
  purrr::chuck("mod")

data_varpart_plot <-
  mod_hmsc_full %>%
  Hmsc::computeVariancePartitioning() %>%
  purrr::chuck("vals") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("factor") %>%
  tibble::as_tibble() %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -factor,
    names_to = "species",
    values_to = "r2"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    factor = dplyr::case_when(
      factor == "bio1" ~ "MAT",
      factor == "bio12" ~ "MAP",
      factor == "Random: age" ~ "Time",
      factor == "Random: dataset_name" ~ "Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    factor_simple = dplyr::case_when(
      .default = factor,
      factor %in% c("Time", "Space") ~ "Random effects",
      factor %in% c("MAT", "MAP") ~ "Abiotic variables"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-varpart-plot-simple
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
data_varpart_plot %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(
      x = species,
      y = r2,
      fill = factor_simple
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = "fill"
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_flip(
    ylim = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Species",
    y = "Proportion of explained variance"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(
      angle = 90,
      vjust = 0.5,
      hjust = 1
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_d()
```

## Variation partitioning

<br>

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-varpart-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
data_varpart_plot %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(
      x = species,
      y = r2,
      fill = factor
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = "fill"
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_flip(
    ylim = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Species",
    y = "Proportion of explained variance"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(
      angle = 90,
      vjust = 0.5,
      hjust = 1
    ),
    axis.ticks.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_d()
```

## Effect sizes

<br>

_How important are the environmental variables in determining species occurrences?_

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-effect-sizes-data
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

mod_hmsc_full_coda <-
  Hmsc::convertToCodaObject(mod_hmsc_full)

vec_all_vars <-
  mod_hmsc_full_coda %>%
  purrr::chuck("Beta", 1) %>%
  colnames()

vec_vars_bio1 <-
  vec_all_vars[
    vec_all_vars %>%
      stringr::str_detect("C2") %>%
      which()
  ]

vec_vars_bio12 <-
  vec_all_vars[
    vec_all_vars %>%
      stringr::str_detect("C3") %>%
      which()
  ]
```

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-effect-sizes-1
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| fig-height: 10

mod_hmsc_full_coda %>%
  purrr::chuck("Beta") %>%
  MCMCplot(
    object = .,
    ci = c(50, 90),
    params = vec_vars_bio1,
    ISB = FALSE,
    ref_ovl = TRUE,
    rank = TRUE,
    main = "Effect sizes for MAT"
  )
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r}
#| label: model-validation-effect-sizes-2
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| fig-height: 10

mod_hmsc_full_coda %>%
  purrr::chuck("Beta") %>%
  MCMCplot(
    ci = c(50, 90),
    params = vec_vars_bio12,
    ISB = FALSE,
    ref_ovl = TRUE,
    rank = TRUE,
    main = "Effect sizes for MAP"
  )
```

:::

::::

## Species associations

<br>

_Which species tend to co-occur more or less often than expected by chance?_

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%" .fragment}
 - Based on the [residual correlation matrix]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} from the HMSC model
:::

::: {.column width="70%" .fragment}
```{r}
#| label: species-associations-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true

data_species_associations_total <-
  targets::tar_read(
    name = "data_species_associations_total",
    store = set_store
  )

data_species_associations_total %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    type == "dataset_name"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    prop_sign_assoc = n_sign_assoc,
    percentage_sign_assoc = round(prop_sign_assoc * 100),
    n_total = 100,
    n_not_sign_assoc = n_total - percentage_sign_assoc
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(percentage_sign_assoc, n_not_sign_assoc) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    group_name = dplyr::case_when(
      group == "percentage_sign_assoc" ~ "Significant associations",
      group == "n_not_sign_assoc" ~ "Non-significant associations"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill = group_name, values = value)
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_equal() +
  waffle::geom_waffle(
    n_rows = 10,
    size = 0.5,
    colour = "white",
    flip = TRUE,
    make_proportional = TRUE
  ) +
  ggplot2::guides(
    fill = ggplot2::guide_legend(
      ncol = 1
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    title = "Percentage of significant co-occurrences"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_void() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.title = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

:::

::::


# Biogeography

<br>

::: {.incremental}

 - We can use the fitted HMSC model to [predict]{.text-background-green .text-bold} species occurrences across space and time
   - e.g., predicting the species [colonization patterns]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} after the Last Glacial Maximum (LGM)

:::

```{r}
#| label: prediction-setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
age_lim <-
  get_active_config(
    value = c("vegvault_data", "age_lim")
  )

vec_age_slices <-
  seq(
    min(age_lim),
    max(age_lim),
    get_active_config(
      value = c("data_processing", "time_step")
    )
  )

x_lim <-
  get_active_config(
    value = c("vegvault_data", "x_lim")
  )

x_lim_max <- max(x_lim, na.rm = TRUE)
x_lim_min <- min(x_lim, na.rm = TRUE)

y_lim <-
  get_active_config(
    value = c("vegvault_data", "y_lim")
  )

y_lim_max <- max(y_lim, na.rm = TRUE)
y_lim_min <- min(y_lim, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: prediction-new-data
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| cache: true
con <-
  DBI::dbConnect(
    RSQLite::SQLite(),
    here::here("Data/Input/VegVault.sqlite")
  )

data_gridpoints_raw <-
  dplyr::tbl(con, "Datasets") %>%
  dplyr::filter(data_source_type_id == 5) %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    coord_long >= x_lim_min &
      coord_long <= x_lim_max &
      coord_lat >= y_lim_min &
      coord_lat <= y_lim_max
  ) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    dplyr::tbl(con, "DatasetSample"),
    by = "dataset_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    dplyr::tbl(con, "Samples"),
    by = "sample_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    dplyr::tbl(con, "AbioticData") %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        abiotic_variable_id == 1 | # BIO1
          abiotic_variable_id == 4 # BIO12
      ),
    by = "sample_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::collect()

DBI::dbDisconnect(con)

```

```{r}
#| label: prediction-new-data-wrangle
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true

data_to_predict <-
  data_gridpoints_raw %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    age %in% vec_age_slices
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    dataset_name,
    coord_long, coord_lat,
    age,
    abiotic_variable_id, abiotic_value
  )


data_to_predict_distinct <-
  data_to_predict %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = abiotic_variable_id,
    values_from = abiotic_value,
    names_prefix = "var_id_"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(coord_long, coord_lat, age, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    bio1 = var_id_1,
    bio12 = var_id_4
  )

data_to_predict_clim <-
  data_to_predict_distinct %>%
  dplyr::select(bio1, bio12) %>%
  as.data.frame()


data_coords <-
  data_to_predict_distinct %>%
  dplyr::distinct(dataset_name, coord_long, coord_lat) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("dataset_name")

random_coors_knots <-
  Hmsc::constructKnots(
    sData = data_coords,
    minKnotDist = get_active_config(
      c("data_processing", "min_distance_of_gpp_knots")
    )
  )

random_level_coords <-
  Hmsc::HmscRandomLevel(
    sData = data_coords,
    sMethod = "GPP",
    sKnot = random_coors_knots
  )

data_age <-
  data_to_predict_distinct %>%
  dplyr::distinct(age) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(age) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    age_rowname = as.character(age)
  ) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("age_rowname")

random_level_age <-
  Hmsc::HmscRandomLevel(
    sData = data_age
  )

study_design <-
  data_to_predict_distinct %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    age_factor = factor(
      x = age,
      levels = sort(unique(as.numeric(age))),
      ordered = TRUE
    ),
    dataset_name_factor = as.factor(dataset_name)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    "dataset_name" = "dataset_name_factor",
    "age" = "age_factor"
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()


```

```{r}
#| label: prediction-new-data-predict
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| cache: false

# There is an issue with caching here (the file is too large), so I am using manual saving/loading

if (
  file.exists(
    here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/_presentation_01_temp/data_pred.rds")
  )
) {
  data_pred <-
    readr::read_rds(
      here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/_presentation_01_temp/data_pred.rds")
    )
} else {
  data_pred <-
    Hmsc:::predict.Hmsc(
      object = mod_hmsc_full,
      XData = data_to_predict_clim,
      studyDesign = study_design,
      ranLevels = list(
        age = random_level_age,
        dataset_name = random_level_coords
      ),
      predictEtaMean = TRUE,
      expected = TRUE
    )

  readr::write_rds(
    x = data_pred,
    file = here::here("Documentation/Reports/First_report/First_presentation/_presentation_01_temp/data_pred.rds")
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: prediction-new-data-postprocess
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| cache: true
mean_with_na <- function(x) {
  mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
}

data_pred_avg <-
  apply(
    abind::abind(data_pred, along = 3),
    c(1, 2),
    FUN = mean_with_na
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: prediction-new-data-get-observed
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true

data_to_fit <-
  targets::tar_read(
    name = "data_to_fit",
    store = set_store
  )

data_taxa_eval <-
  tibble::tibble(
    taxon = data_to_fit %>%
      purrr::chuck("data_community_to_fit") %>%
      colnames(),
    mod_hmsc_full_with_eval %>%
      purrr::chuck("eval") %>%
      as.data.frame()
  )

data_observed <-
  data_to_fit %>%
  purrr::chuck("data_community_to_fit") %>%
  add_age_column_from_rownames() %>%
  add_dataset_name_column_from_rownames() %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    data_to_fit %>%
      purrr::chuck("data_coords_to_fit") %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("dataset_name"),
    by = "dataset_name"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(age, dataset_name, coord_long, coord_lat),
    names_to = "taxon",
    values_to = "proportion"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    present = ifelse(proportion > 0, "present", "absent")
  )

data_predicted <-
  as_tibble(study_design) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    coord_long = dataset_name %>%
      stringr::str_remove("geo_") %>%
      stringr::str_remove("_.*"),
    coord_lat = dataset_name %>%
      stringr::str_remove("geo_") %>%
      stringr::str_remove(".*_"),
    age = as.numeric(as.character(age))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::bind_cols(
    as_tibble(data_pred_avg)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(dataset_name, coord_lat, coord_long, age),
    names_to = "taxon",
    values_to = "pred_value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(taxon, coord_long, coord_lat, age, pred_value)
```

## Single taxa plot

```{r}
#| label: prediction-new-data-plot-single-taxa
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| out-width: "100%"
#| out-height: "100%"
#| fig-height: 6
sel_taxa <- "Fraxinus"

data_observed_sel_taxa <-
  data_observed %>%
  dplyr::filter(taxon == sel_taxa)

data_predicted_sel_taxa <-
  data_predicted %>%
  dplyr::filter(taxon == sel_taxa)

# Make a plot
data_predicted_sel_taxa %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot() +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~age) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(
      x = as.numeric(coord_long),
      y = as.numeric(coord_lat),
      fill = pred_value
    ),
    col = NA,
    alpha = 0.8,
    linewidth = 0,
    width = 0.5,
    height = 0.5
  ) +
  ggplot2::borders(
    "world",
    fill = NA,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    data = data_observed_sel_taxa,
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(
      x = as.numeric(coord_long),
      y = as.numeric(coord_lat),
      color = as.factor(present),
      shape = as.factor(present)
    ),
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "present" = "red",
      "absent" = "black"
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(
      "present" = 12,
      "absent" = 13
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_quickmap(
    xlim = x_lim,
    ylim = y_lim
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    fill = "Predicted\nprobability of\noccurrence",
    color = "Observed",
    shape = "Observed",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    title = paste(
      "Predicted occurrence of",
      sel_taxa,
      char_taxonomic_resolution,
      " for Czechia"
    ),
    subtitle = "Each facet is a different time slice",
    caption = paste(
      paste(
        "HMSC model with space (GPP; minimum distance of",
        get_active_config(
          c("data_processing", "min_distance_of_gpp_knots")
        ),
        ") and age as random effect"
      ),
      paste(
        "Climatic variables:",
        char_abiotic_vars
      ),
      paste(
        "Model fitted with",
        n_samples_total,
        "samples total and",
        n_validation_folds,
        "cross-validation folds"
      ),
      paste(
        "Model evaluation:",
        data_taxa_eval %>%
          dplyr::filter(taxon == sel_taxa) %>%
          dplyr::pull(AUC) %>%
          round(3),
        "AUC;",
        data_taxa_eval %>%
          dplyr::filter(taxon == sel_taxa) %>%
          dplyr::pull(TjurR2) %>%
          round(3),
        "TjurR2;",
        data_taxa_eval %>%
          dplyr::filter(taxon == sel_taxa) %>%
          dplyr::pull(RMSE) %>%
          round(3),
        "RMSE"
      ),
      sep = "\n"
    )
  )

```

## Diversity through time

```{r}
#| label: prediction-diversity-estimation
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false

data_predicted_richness <-
  seq(0.25, 0.75, by = 0.05) %>%
  rlang::set_names() %>%
  purrr::map(
    .f = ~ data_predicted %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        present = ifelse(pred_value >= .x, TRUE, FALSE)
      ) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(coord_long, coord_lat, age) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        richness = sum(present, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "threshold") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(coord_long, coord_lat, age) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    richness_mean = mean(richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    richness_min = min(richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    richness_max = max(richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    richness_range = abs(richness_max - richness_min),
    richness_sd = sd(richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: prediction-diversity-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| out-width: "100%"
#| out-height: "100%"
#| fig-height: 6

data_predicted_richness %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot() +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~age) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(
      x = as.numeric(coord_long),
      y = as.numeric(coord_lat),
      fill = richness_mean,
      alpha = 1 / richness_range
    ),
    col = NA,
    linewidth = 0,
    width = 0.25,
    height = 0.25
  ) +
  ggplot2::borders(
    "world",
    fill = NA,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(
    limits = c(
      14,
      15.5
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_alpha_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_quickmap(
    xlim = x_lim,
    ylim = y_lim
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    fill = "Predicted\nrichness",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    alpha = "Prediction confidence",
    title = paste(
      "Predicted taxonomic richness for Czechia"
    ),
    subtitle = "Each facet is a different time slice",
    caption = paste(
      paste(
        "HMSC model with space (GPP; minimum distance of",
        get_active_config(
          c("data_processing", "min_distance_of_gpp_knots")
        ),
        ") and age as random effect"
      ),
      paste(
        "Climatic variables:",
        char_abiotic_vars
      ),
      paste(
        "Model fitted with",
        n_cores,
        "chains,",
        n_samples_total,
        "samples total,",
        n_validation_folds,
        "cross-validation folds"
      ),
      sep = "\n"
    )
  )

```

# Time pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-0
```

## Time pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-time-1
flowchart LR
    Czechia-.->Basic_pipeline
    subgraph Project
    Czechia
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
    Basic_pipeline
    end
```

## Time pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-time-2
flowchart LR
    Czechia-.->Time_pipeline
    subgraph Project
    Czechia
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
    Time_pipeline
    end
```

## Time pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-basic-3
```

## Time pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-time-3
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Data_subset
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_subset
        species_associations_1-->results
        species_associations_2-->results
        species_associations_3-->results
        subgraph Age_slices
            direction LR
            Data_subset-->model_age_1
            Data_subset-->model_age_2
            Data_subset-->model_age_3
            model_age_1-->species_associations_1
            model_age_2-->species_associations_2
            model_age_3-->species_associations_3
        end
end
```

## Time pipeline

<br>
<br>

```{mermaid}
%%| label: mermaid-pipeline-time-4
flowchart LR
    Config-.->VegVault
    Config-.->Classification
    Config-.->Data_interpolated
    Config-.->Data_subset
    subgraph Project
        direction TB
        Config
    end
    subgraph Pipeline
        direction LR
        VegVault-->Data_community
        VegVault-->Data_abiotic
        Data_community-->Classification
        Classification-->Data_harmonised
        Data_abiotic-->Data_interpolated
        Data_harmonised-->Data_interpolated
        Data_interpolated-->Data_subset
        species_associations_1-->results
        species_associations_2-->results
        species_associations_3-->results
        subgraph Age_slices
            direction LR
            Data_subset-->model_age_1
            Data_subset-->model_age_2
            Data_subset-->model_age_3
            model_age_1-->species_associations_1
            model_age_2-->species_associations_2
            model_age_3-->species_associations_3
        end
end
```

## R implementation

<br>

```{r}
#| label: target-graph-time
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| output: false
Sys.setenv(R_CONFIG_ACTIVE = "project_cz")
vec_pipelines <- "pipeline_time"

set_store <-
  vec_pipelines %>%
  paste0(
    get_active_config("target_store"), "/", ., "/"
  ) %>%
  here::here()

plot_target_graph_time <-
  targets::tar_visnetwork(
    script = paste0(
      "R/02_Main_analyses", "/", vec_pipelines, ".R"
    ) %>%
      here::here(),
    store = set_store,
    outdated = FALSE,
    targets_only = TRUE,
    physics = TRUE,
    level_separation = 150
  )

```


```{r}
#| label: target-graph-time-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| output: true
plot_target_graph_time
```

## Co-occurrences in time slices

<br>

```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-time-slices-waffle
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true

data_species_associations_by_age <-
  targets::tar_read(
    name = "data_species_associations_by_age",
    store = set_store
  )

data_species_associations_by_age_to_plot <-
  data_species_associations_by_age %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    percentage_sign_assoc = round(prop_sign_assoc * 100),
    n_total = 100,
    n_not_sign_assoc = n_total - percentage_sign_assoc
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(percentage_sign_assoc, n_not_sign_assoc, age) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -age,
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    group_name = dplyr::case_when(
      group == "percentage_sign_assoc" ~ "Significant associations",
      group == "n_not_sign_assoc" ~ "Non-significant associations"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill = group_name, values = value)
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_equal() +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~age) +
  waffle::geom_waffle(
    n_rows = 10,
    size = 0.5,
    colour = "white",
    flip = TRUE,
    make_proportional = TRUE
  ) +
  ggplot2::guides(
    fill = ggplot2::guide_legend(
      ncol = 1
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    title = "Percentage of significant co-occurrences"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_void() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.title = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  
```

```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-time-slices-waffle-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
data_species_associations_by_age_to_plot

```

## Co-occurrences in time slices

<br>

```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-time-slices-line-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true

targets::tar_read(
  name = "plot_species_associations",
  store = set_store
)

```

# Next steps

<br>

_There is so much more to do! Here are some ideas for [next steps]{.text-background-blue .text-bold}._

## Technical improvements

<br>

::: {.incremental}

- Validate the mode with other [framework for JSDM]{.text-background-blue .text-bold}
  - {spAbundance} or {spOccupancy}
- Filter out climatic variables before fitting (based on correlation)
- [Speed everything up]{.text-background-green .text-bold}
  - Set up HPC at Metacentrum
  - use scalable joint Species Distribution Model ({sjSDM})?

::: 

## Methodological improvements

<br>

::: {.incremental}

- Move beyond presence-absence data 
  - use the [hurdle model]{.text-background-blue .text-bold}
- Implement [functional traits]{.text-background-green .text-bold} in the model
- Implement the functional types classification
- Get more nuanced understanding of species associations by analysing co-occurrences as [bipartite networks]{.text-background-brown .text-bold} (food webs)

:::

## Co-occurrences as bipartite networks

<br>

::: {.incremental}
 - Co-occurrence networks can be seen as [bipartite networks]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} 
  - first layer: [individual plots]{.text-background-green .text-bold}
  - second layer: [taxa]{.text-background-brown .text-bold}

:::

```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-bipartite-setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
#| cache: true
sel_age <- 2000

data_community <-
  data_to_fit %>%
  purrr::chuck("data_community_to_fit") %>%
  add_dataset_name_column_from_rownames() %>%
  add_age_column_from_rownames() %>%
  tibble::as_tibble()

data_community_timeslice <-
  data_community %>%
  dplyr::filter(age == sel_age) %>%
  dplyr::select(-age) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("dataset_name")

data_community_timeslice_binary <-
  data_community_timeslice

data_community_timeslice_binary[data_community_timeslice_binary > 0] <- 1
```

## Co-occurrences as bipartite networks

<br>

Example of time slice `r sel_age ` years BP:
```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-bipartite-example-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| cache: true
data_community_timeslice_binary %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  bipartite::plotweb(
    text_size = 1,
    sorting = "dec",
    empty = TRUE,
    srt = 90,
    lower_labels = FALSE
  )

```

## Co-occurrences as bipartite networks

<br>

Calculate network-level metrics for each network:

```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-bipartite
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| cache: true

vec_ages <-
  data_community %>%
  dplyr::pull(age) %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort() %>%
  rlang::set_names()

list_data_community_binary <-
  purrr::map(
    .x = vec_ages,
    .f = ~ {
      data_community_timeslice <-
        data_community %>%
        dplyr::filter(age == .x) %>%
        dplyr::select(-age) %>%
        tibble::column_to_rownames("dataset_name")

      data_community_timeslice_binary <-
        data_community_timeslice

      data_community_timeslice_binary[data_community_timeslice_binary > 0] <- 1

      data_community_timeslice_binary %>%
        as.matrix() %>%
        return()
    }
  )

set.seed(1234)
data_network_metrics <-
  purrr::map(
    .progress = TRUE,
    .x = list_data_community_binary,
    .f = ~ bipartite::networklevel(.x) %>%
      as.data.frame() %>%
      rlang::set_names("value") %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("metric") %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(
        names_from = metric,
        values_from = value
      ) %>%
      janitor::clean_names()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "age")

```

```{r}
#| label: cooccurrence-bipartite-plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: true
data_network_metrics %>%
  dplyr::select(
    age,
    connectance,
    nestedness,
    modularity_q
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -age,
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    metric_name = dplyr::case_when(
      metric == "connectance" ~ "Connectance",
      metric == "nestedness" ~ "Nestedness",
      metric == "modularity_q" ~ "Modularity (Q)",
      TRUE ~ metric
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    mapping = ggplot2::aes(
      x = as.numeric(age),
      y = value
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_line() +
  ggplot2::geom_point() +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~metric_name, scales = "free_y") +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
    transform = "reverse"
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Age (cal yr BP)",
    y = "Metric value",
    title = "Selected metrics through time"
  )
```

# Open and public {.bg-green}

```{r}
#| label: generate-urls
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
url_gh <-
    paste0(
    "https://github.com/OndrejMottl/",
    "BIODYNAMICS-vegetation-cooccurrence"
  )
```

<br>

Everything shown here is fully [open]{.text-background-blue .text-bold} and [reproducible]{.text-background-brown .text-bold}! You can find the whole project( inlcuding code and materials for this presentation) on GitHub:

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}
[😸 Code on GitHub](`r url_gh`)

[![](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
```{r}
#| label: qr_slides
qr_slides <-
  qrcode::qr_code(
    url_gh,
    ecl = "H"
  )

qrcode::generate_svg(
  qrcode = qr_slides,
  filename = here::here("Documentation/Materials/QR/qr_slides.svg"),
  foreground = colors['black'],
  background = colors['green'],
)

include_local_figure("QR/qr_slides.svg")
```

:::

::::
